EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 105 TO 110 MPH TOUCHED DOWN NEAR QUAIL DRIVE AND LIFTED NEAR CLOESSNER DRIVE. EMC REPORTS 10 HOMES DAMAGED, SEVERAL LOST ROOFS, A COUPLE HAD TREES FALL ON THEM. BOAT HOUSES AND GARAGES WERE ALSO SEVERELY DAMAGED IN THE AREA.

TORNADO STARTED NEAR A JAMES ROAD THEN CROSSED HIGHWAY 121 AND DAMAGE PROPERTY ON DRAKE ROAD WITH SEVERAL PINES TREES DAMAGED AND A METAL BUILDING HAD ITS ROOF TORN OFF AND THROWN FOR SEVERAL YARDS. ON THE NEXT PRIVATE ROAD OVER, THERE WAS MINOR DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF A MOBILE HOME. THE LAST VISIBLE DAMAGE WAS NEAR ALLEN GORDY ROAD WITH TREE DAMAGE.

TORNADO CONTINUED NE FOR 4 MINUTES WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. TWO HOUSES AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS SUSTAINED DAMAGE.

TORNADO CONTINUED NE FOR 11 MINUTES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS. DAMAGE TO A LARGE OFFICE SUPPLY BUILDING. DAMAGE TO THE LSU AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY OFFICE.
PICTURE OF TORNADO IN A FIELD JUST WEST OF LA 29. SURVEY TEAM SURVEYED THE AREA AND FOUND THE EXACT LOCATION IN THE PHOTO, HOWEVER THE TORNADO WAS IN AN MARSHY AREA AND WILL BE RATED EF UNKOWN.

TORNADO DAMAGED NUMEROUS HOMES, OUTBUILDS AND THE HOSPITAL. TORNADO WAS 4.5 MILES LONG AND 300 YARDS WIDE.

PHOTOS RECEIVED OF STORM DAMAGE ALONG FM1943 WEST OF FRED, TX. SEVERAL TREES KNOCKED DOWN OR UPROOTED, IT APPEARS METAL SHEETING OR SIDING WAS PULLED FROM AN AWNING OR BUILDING AND A PONTOON BOAT WAS OVERTURNED OR THROWN. POTENTIAL TORNADO. TIME AND EXACT LOCATION APPROXIMATED BY RADAR. A STORM SURVEY HAS BEEN PLANNED FOR TOMORROW MORNING, FRIDAY DECEMBER 30, 2022.

RESULTS OF A STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 90 MPH. THE TORNADO DAMAGED 3 MOBILE HOMES, THE AWNING OF A BUILDING, SNAPPED A FEW PINE TREES AT THE TRUNK, AND UPROOTED SOME HARDWOOD TREES.
